meta.feed_title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

items.title: Video: Teachers in northern Nigeria protest abduction of school children
items.summary: Teachers in north-eastern Nigeria held protests, demanding stronger protection for learning institutions.

items.title: Ferrari sign Leclerc on new long-term contract before home F1 Monaco GP
items.summary: Charles Leclerc has extended his contract with Ferrari for an undisclosed length, having joined the F1 team in 2019.

items.title: Israel is building more military posts in Gaza, satellite imagery shows
items.summary: An investigation by Al Jazeera has identified 40 distinct Israeli military outposts entrenched within Gaza.

items.title: Iran, Kuwait, Bahrain hit: Is the war in the Gulf escalating again?
items.summary: Secretary of State Rubio tells US lawmakers the war on Iran is over, amid recent attacks on Iran and Gulf countries.

items.title: Morocco World Cup 2026 preview: Players to watch, group and squad list
items.summary: The Atlas Lions aim to better their historic 2022 semifinal appearance but have had a chaotic run-in.

items.title: Israel’s invasion of southern Lebanon devastates centuries of history
items.summary: From Phoenician temples to Crusader castles, heritage sites bear the brunt of Israel's expanding military offensive.

items.title: Cape Verde football team arrives in the US for the World Cup
items.summary: Cape Verde’s national football team has arrived in the US ahead to take part in their first ever FIFA World Cup.

items.title: US artist sues FIFA over destruction of Dallas whale mural for World Cup
items.summary: Wyland's mural stood for nearly three decades before workers began painting over it last month to promote the World Cup.

items.title: Hilton, Becerra lead California governor race: Key primary election results
items.summary: California, Iowa, New Jersey, New Mexico, Montana and South Dakota held key primaries on Tuesday.

items.title: Fire at New Delhi hotel kills at least 21 people
items.summary: It took eight fire engines to extinguish the blaze, as more than 40 people were rescued and taken to nearby hospitals.

items.title: Remembering Muhammad Ali’s message of peace
items.summary: Ten years after his passing, Ali’s words remind us of what we must do amid today’s conflicts and divisions.

items.title: How social media changes the way we see war
items.summary: We see war now as we see everything else - through a screen.

items.title: Hezbollah video shows attack on Israeli troops at Lebanon’s Beaufort Castle
items.summary: Hezbollah has released drone video showing attacks targeting Israeli forces occupying Beaufort Castle

items.title: Democrat and Republican go head-to-head in California governor race
items.summary: California could have its first Republican governor since Arnold Schwarzenegger ended his two terms in 2011.

items.title: US ‘disables’ ship allegedly bound for Iranian port

items.title: Foreign nationals among 21 dead in fire in Indian capital
items.summary: Firefighters rescue more than 40 people trapped inside as thick smoke fills the burning New Delhi building.

items.summary: Ten years after the boxing legend’s death, widow Lonnie Ali urges a 'day of compassion' as US divisions deepen.

items.summary: Veteran journalist's dismissal comes after he reportedly accused management of 'murdering' influential news programme.

items.title: Outrage over UK police arrest video of dying student
